instructions.purpose: Retrieve the full list of currently scheduled cron jobs. Use this tool to discover what reminders and recurring tasks are active, so you can inform the user about them or follow up with an unschedule-recurring call if the user wants to remove them.

tool.name: list-cron-jobs
tool.description: Returns all scheduled cron jobs with their name, group, type, pattern, next fire time, last run time, and creation time. Takes no parameters.

usage_rules.rule: Call this tool whenever the user asks about their current reminders, scheduled tasks, or active cron jobs
After receiving the results, present each job clearly to the user — include the name, schedule pattern, and next fire time
If the user wants to remove one or more jobs, use the unschedule-recurring tool with the exact job name
If there are no jobs, inform the user that no scheduled jobs exist
Do not call this tool repeatedly in a short time span — cache the results within a conversation turn

example.context: User says: "What reminders do I have set up?"
example.reasoning: User wants to know about their active cron jobs. Call list-cron-jobs with no parameters.
First list all jobs to see what exists, then call unschedule-recurring for each one the user confirms.

example.context: User says: "Do I have any scheduled tasks?"
example.reasoning: User is checking if any cron jobs exist. Call list-cron-jobs to find out.
